  The Dataset template provides a consistent and accessible framework for presenting official statistics. The Dataset template provides users with access to published datasets, supporting information and downloadable data files in a consistent and structured format.

The template is designed to help users quickly access the latest available data while also providing clear links to methodology, related content and previous versions where relevant. It supports both simple data retrieval and deeper exploration of the context surrounding a dataset.

This template is used for published datasets across the ONS website.

## Preview

  embedHeight={900}

  devModeLink="https://www.figma.com/design/iovDnFhotzD7ETK3RXADos/ONS-Interaction-Design-Library?node-id=477-648&m=dev&t=AauwUuST4kaR7tMT-1"
/>

## Purpose and goals

The Dataset template enables users to find, understand and download the latest version of a dataset. It brings together dataset series and dataset edition content into a single page, reducing unnecessary navigation and helping users access data more quickly.

Research showed that users most often want to reach the latest available data. Previously, dataset series pages acted primarily as a navigational step to dataset editions. Combining these views into a single template creates a more direct and streamlined journey.

The template also provides clear pathways to supporting information, related content and previous versions, helping users understand the wider context and lifecycle of a dataset.

This template aims to:
* Present dataset information in a consistent and structured format
* Reduce confusion between editions and versions
* Improve confidence in accessing and understanding data
* Support efficient download of data files
* Connect datasets to related analysis, methodology and supporting content

## Structure and key features

The Dataset template follows the standard ONS content page layout and is composed of several key sections.

### Phase banner

The page includes the Phase Banner component to indicate that the service is currently in beta.

### Site header

The standard Site Header component provides access to site navigation and search.

### Page banner

The Page Banner introduces the dataset and includes:
* The dataset title
* A short description explaining the content and purpose of the dataset

### Table of contents

A table of contents displayed in the left sidebar allows users to navigate between sections of the page.

## Dataset content

### Edition metadata

The metadata section provides key information about the dataset edition being viewed.

This may include:
* Release date
* Next release date
* Contact information
* Version number
* Total number of versions
* Accreditation status

This information helps users understand how up to date the dataset is, its status and who is responsible for it.

### Get the data

The Get the data section allows users to download the dataset in available formats, such as Excel, CSV or TXT.

Download options use the File Download component to provide a consistent and accessible download experience.

Each download option should clearly indicate:
* File format
* File size
* A short description of the file contents where needed

Only formats that are available should be shown.

### Quality and methodology information

This section links to supporting methodology content and explains what information it contains.

Methodology information helps users understand:
* How the data was collected
* How the dataset should be interpreted
* Any limitations or important considerations

### Important notes and usage information (optional)

This optional section provides additional guidance to help users understand and use the dataset appropriately.

It may include:
* Caveats or limitations
* Changes in methodology
* Advice on interpretation or reuse

This section should only be included where additional context is necessary.

### Related content

The Related content section helps users explore content connected to the dataset.

This may include:
* Statistical articles
* Related datasets
* Previous or related editions
* Methodology or guidance pages
* Dashboards or data visualisations

This section supports discoverability and helps connect users to relevant analysis and supporting information.

### Version history

Where a dataset edition has undergone revisions or corrections, a version history is shown to maintain transparency.

Previous versions should remain accessible and are displayed in reverse chronological order.

The Version History component may also be reused across other content types where version tracking is required.

### Contact details

The Contact Details component provides users with details for the responsible statistical team.

This may include:
* Team name
* Email address
* Telephone number

This component is reused across multiple content types to provide a consistent contact experience.

## Future considerations

### Edition selector

Earlier versions of the dataset journey separated dataset series pages from dataset edition pages. In this model, users selected an edition from a dedicated series page.

The current template combines these experiences into a single page focused on the latest available edition.

An edition selector is not included in the initial Phase 2 release because only a single edition will be available. Including a navigational component for edition switching would therefore add unnecessary complexity.

Further research and requirements gathering are needed to determine whether an edition selector is required in future iterations, and how users should navigate between historical editions where relevant.
